--- > [!IMPORTANT] > AI Review is run only on demand for your team. We're only showing results of static analysis review right now. To trigger AI Review, comment `@deepsourcebot review` on this thread.

## Reviewer's Guide Reverts SignalBroadcaster payload types back to zero-allocation structs and removes fail-fast exception rethrows in safety-critical flatten and stop-sync flows so processing continues after unexpected errors while preserving critical logging. #### Flow diagram for updated flatten/stop error handling ```mermaid flowchart TD A[Start flatten or stop operation] --> B[Process next account or position] B --> C{Exception thrown?} C -- No --> D[Continue normal processing] D --> E[More work?] E -- Yes --> B E -- No --> F[Operation complete] C -- Yes --> G[Log CRITICAL error with Print] G --> H[Release guards or state if needed] H --> E ``` ### File-Level Changes | Change | Details | Files | | ------ | ------- | ----- | | Restore zero-allocation signal payloads by reverting signal types from classes back to structs. |
  • Changed all signal payload types (TradeSignal, TrailUpdateSignal, StopUpdateSignal, EntryUpdateSignal, OrderCancelSignal, TargetActionSignal, FlattenSignal, BreakevenSignal, ExternalCommandSignal) from class to struct.
  • Removed inheritance from EventArgs for all signal payload types to decouple from the .NET EventArgs pattern.
  • Updated XML documentation comments to describe the zero-allocation intent instead of class/EventArgs conversion notes.
| `src/SignalBroadcaster.cs` | | Stop failing fast on unexpected errors during SIMA flatten operations while preserving CRITICAL logging and guard handling. |
  • In FlattenAllApexAccounts, ChainNextFlattenOp, and ClosePositionsOnlyApexAccounts, kept guard-release logic but removed generic catch rethrows so remaining accounts still get flatten attempts.
  • In PumpFlattenOps and EmergencyFlattenSingleFleetAccount, retained detailed CRITICAL logging but stopped rethrowing to allow the flatten pump to continue for other accounts.
  • Adjusted comments on all affected catch blocks to clarify logging-only behavior and rationale for not rethrowing.
| `src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs` | | Prevent flatten pipeline from aborting across fleet accounts on unexpected errors in order management flatten routines. |
  • In ManageCIT, removed the rethrow from the generic catch while keeping CRITICAL log output so failures for one key do not stop others from being processed.
  • In FlattenAll, removed the rethrow from the generic catch while preserving CRITICAL logging and finally cleanup so the overall flatten flow can continue where possible.
| `src/V12_002.Orders.Management.Flatten.cs` | | Avoid aborting stop synchronization on errors so positions remain protected as much as possible. |
  • In UpdateStopQuantity, removed the rethrow from the generic catch while keeping CRITICAL logging about potential unprotected positions.
  • In CreateNewStopOrder, removed the rethrow while preserving CRITICAL logging so stop creation attempts continue despite unexpected errors.
| `src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs` | ---
Tips and commands #### Interacting with Sourcery - **Trigger a new review:** Comment `@sourcery-ai review` on the pull request. - **Continue discussions:** Reply directly to Sourcery's review comments. - **Generate a GitHub issue from a review comment:** Ask Sourcery to create an issue from a review comment by replying to it. You can also reply to a review comment with `@sourcery-ai issue` to create an issue from it. - **Generate a pull request title:** Write `@sourcery-ai` anywhere in the pull request title to generate a title at any time. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai title` on the pull request to (re-)generate the title at any time. - **Generate a pull request summary:** Write `@sourcery-ai summary` anywhere in the pull request body to generate a PR summary at any time exactly where you want it. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai summary` on the pull request to (re-)generate the summary at any time. - **Generate reviewer's guide:** Comment `@sourcery-ai guide` on the pull request to (re-)generate the reviewer's guide at any time. - **Resolve all Sourcery comments:** Comment `@sourcery-ai resolve` on the pull request to resolve all Sourcery comments. Useful if you've already addressed all the comments and don't want to see them anymore. - **Dismiss all Sourcery reviews:** Comment `@sourcery-ai dismiss` on the pull request to dismiss all existing Sourcery reviews. Especially useful if you want to start fresh with a new review - don't forget to comment `@sourcery-ai review` to trigger a new review! #### Customizing Your Experience Access your [dashboard](https://app.sourcery.ai) to: - Enable or disable review features such as the Sourcery-generated pull request summary, the reviewer's guide, and others. - Change the review language. - Add, remove or edit custom review instructions. - Adjust other review settings. #### Getting Help - [Contact our support team](mailto:support@sourcery.ai) for questions or feedback. - Visit our [documentation](https://docs.sourcery.ai) for detailed guides and information. - Keep in touch with the Sourcery team by following us on [X/Twitter](https://x.com/SourceryAI), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/sourcery-ai/) or [GitHub](https://github.com/sourcery-ai).

Code Review Ô£à Approved 1 resolved / 1 findings Removes the incompatible `where T : EventArgs` constraint from `SafeInvoke` and suppresses `IEquatable` warnings, successfully enabling the use of zero-allocation struct signal payloads.
Ô£à 1 resolved
Ô£à Bug: Structs don't satisfy where T : EventArgs constraint - won't compile > ­ƒôä src/SignalBroadcaster.cs:18 ­ƒôä src/SignalBroadcaster.cs:52 ­ƒôä src/SignalBroadcaster.cs:65 ­ƒôä src/SignalBroadcaster.cs:78 ­ƒôä src/SignalBroadcaster.cs:90 ­ƒôä src/SignalBroadcaster.cs:101 ­ƒôä src/SignalBroadcaster.cs:128 ­ƒôä src/SignalBroadcaster.cs:138 ­ƒôä src/SignalBroadcaster.cs:149 ­ƒôä src/SignalBroadcaster.cs:225-226 ­ƒôä src/SignalBroadcaster.cs:164-178 > The 9 signal types were reverted to `struct` but `SafeInvoke` at line 225-226 still has the generic constraint `where T : EventArgs`. Since structs cannot inherit from a class, none of the signal types (`TradeSignal`, `TrailUpdateSignal`, etc.) satisfy this constraint. Every `SafeInvoke(...)` call site (lines 275, 289, 304, etc.) will fail to compile. > > Additionally, the event declarations (lines 164-212) still use `EventHandler` which itself has the constraint `where TEventArgs : EventArgs`, so even the event field declarations won't compile with struct types. > > To fix this properly, you need to either: > 1. Replace `EventHandler` with a custom delegate (`Action` or a custom `delegate void SignalHandler(T signal)`) and remove the `where T : EventArgs` constraint from `SafeInvoke`, OR > 2. Keep the types as classes inheriting EventArgs (reverting this PR's change). > > Option 1 aligns with the zero-allocation goal while fixing the compilation error.

reviews.body:
All critical V12 DNA violations from PR #17 have been correctly fixed. The changes restore zero-allocation struct semantics and remove fail-fast exception rethrows in position safety paths, ensuring fleet accounts complete flatten operations even when individual accounts encounter errors. No blocking defects found.

---
You can now have the agent implement changes and create commits directly on your pull request's source branch. Simply comment with /q followed by your request in natural language to ask the agent to make changes.

## Code Review

This pull request converts several signal types in `SignalBroadcaster.cs` from classes to zero-allocation structs for performance alignment, and removes exception rethrowing in various flattening and stop-synchronization catch blocks to ensure remaining accounts are processed. However, the review identifies critical issues: the struct conversion violates `.NET Framework 4.8` constraints on `EventHandler<T>` and `SafeInvoke<T>`, which will cause compilation failures. Additionally, swallowing exceptions in `CreateNewStopOrder` leaves positions unprotected without an emergency flatten, and failing to drain the pending operations queue on failure in `FlattenAllApexAccounts` can result in stale work items.

Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- For the reverted signal payloads, consider making the structs `readonly` (and potentially using init-only setters) to avoid unintended mutation and reduce copying overhead when theyÔÇÖre passed around.
- Now that the generic `catch (Exception)` blocks no longer rethrow, verify whether any upstream logic was relying on these exceptions to trigger recovery/alert paths and, if so, centralize that behavior here instead of silently swallowing after logging.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- For the reverted signal payloads, consider making the structs `readonly` (and potentially using init-only setters) to avoid unintended mutation and reduce copying overhead when theyÔÇÖre passed around.
- Now that the generic `catch (Exception)` blocks no longer rethrow, verify whether any upstream logic was relying on these exceptions to trigger recovery/alert paths and, if so, centralize that behavior here instead of silently swallowing after logging.
~~~

</details>

***

<details>
<summary>Sourcery is free for open source - if you like our reviews please consider sharing them Ô£¿</summary>

- [X](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=I%20just%20got%20an%20instant%20code%20review%20from%20%40SourceryAI%2C%20and%20it%20was%20brilliant%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20open%20source%20and%20has%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20private%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%20https%3A//sourcery.ai)
- [Mastodon](https://mastodon.social/share?text=I%20just%20got%20an%20instant%20code%20review%20from%20%40SourceryAI%2C%20and%20it%20was%20brilliant%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20open%20source%20and%20has%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20private%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%20https%3A//sourcery.ai)
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https://sourcery.ai)

</details>

<sub>
Help me be more useful! Please click ­ƒæì or ­ƒæÄ on each comment and I'll use the feedback to improve your reviews.
</sub>

### Pull Request Overview While the PR correctly attempts to align with zero-allocation performance goals and fleet-wide safety requirements, the current implementation contains a critical compilation error for environments using .NET Framework 4.8 (such as NinjaTrader 8). Converting signal types to structs violates the 'T : EventArgs' constraint of EventHandler. Additionally, the 160-byte size of the TradeSignal struct combined with a lack of IEquatable implementation may inadvertently introduce performance bottlenecks through stack copying and reflection-based boxing, contradicting the primary performance intent. The removal of rethrows in safety paths successfully prevents individual failures from aborting the entire fleet operation. however, the implementation in FlattenAll (V12_002.Orders.Management.Flatten.cs) contains a logic gap where an early exception still prevents subsequent critical steps from executing. Furthermore, swallowing base Exceptions without filters (like OutOfMemoryException) poses a risk to system stability. These issues should be addressed prior to merging to ensure both reliability and performance targets are met. #### About this PR - The removal of 'throw' in generic catch blocks ensures continuity but may mask underlying logic errors if logs are not actively monitored. This is a known trade-off for safety-critical paths, but requires rigorous logging hygiene.
1 comment outside of the diff
src/SignalBroadcaster.cs > `line 164` :red_circle: HIGH RISK > Change the signature of this event handler to use Action instead of EventHandler to accommodate the change to a struct and avoid the EventArgs constraint. > > ```suggestion > public static event Action OnTradeSignal; > ```
#### Test suggestions - [ ] Verify SignalBroadcaster signal types are structs and do not inherit from EventArgs to prevent heap allocations. - [ ] Verify ManageCIT continues processing remaining accounts when an exception occurs during one account's nudge. - [ ] Verify FlattenAll continues fleet-wide execution even if a sub-component (like CancelMasterEntryOrders) throws an unexpected exception. - [ ] Verify UpdateStopQuantity does not rethrow, ensuring position safety checks can continue for other positions. - [ ] Verify SIMA PumpFlattenOps logs errors but proceeds to the next account in the queue via ChainNextFlattenOp.
Prompt proposal for missing tests ``` Consider implementing these tests if applicable: 1. Verify SignalBroadcaster signal types are structs and do not inherit from EventArgs to prevent heap allocations. 2. Verify ManageCIT continues processing remaining accounts when an exception occurs during one account's nudge. 3. Verify FlattenAll continues fleet-wide execution even if a sub-component (like CancelMasterEntryOrders) throws an unexpected exception. 4. Verify UpdateStopQuantity does not rethrow, ensuring position safety checks can continue for other positions. 5. Verify SIMA PumpFlattenOps logs errors but proceeds to the next account in the queue via ChainNextFlattenOp. ```
Low confidence findings - SignalBroadcaster uses EventHandler with structs; depending on the target .NET version, this may trigger boxing or require a custom delegate to remain truly zero-allocation.
